subject: Goal Queue — shared work discovery layer for chain-based multi-agent coordination
title: Goal Queue — Multi-Agent Chain Discovery and Selection
what: Defines the Goal Queue: the shared view of available goal chains that agents query to discover work. The queue is a queryable projection over bytype/chain/ lugs (and the wai_claims Supabase table) filtered to unclaimed, non-expired chains ordered by priority. Agents query the queue at session start, select a chain, claim it, and plan against it. The queue exposes: all available chains, their current claim status, TTL expiry time, and execution_mode. It does NOT store state — it is a live view, not a persistent list.
why: Goal chains (spec-goal-chain-v1) define the unit of work. The Goal Queue is how agents find chains to work on without duplicating effort or requiring a coordinator. Any session — Ozi nightly, AP, or a fresh user session — can query the queue, select the highest-priority available chain, and claim it atomically. Without a queue abstraction, agents would need to scan bytype/chain/ directly and resolve claim conflicts themselves, leading to race conditions and brittle session startup logic.
audience: AP — queries queue at session start to find the highest-priority available chain
Ozi — queries queue nightly to dispatch chains across available session budget
Navigator — reads queue to understand current workload when building portfolio slice
Any session startup routine that needs to find and claim work
use_cases: Session startup: AP queries queue, gets top 3 unclaimed chains ordered by priority, selects one, claims it atomically
Ozi nightly dispatch: queries queue for all unclaimed chains, assigns across available Ozi sessions in priority order
Multi-Ozi coordination: two Ozi instances query simultaneously; first claim wins (Supabase PK); second sees the chain as claimed and skips to next
Stale claim detection: queue query returns chains with expires_at in the past as 'available with stale claim' — enables TTL sweep trigger
Navigator portfolio: Navigator reads queue depth (unclaimed chain count) when deciding whether to recommend new work vs completing existing chains
patterns: Queue is a view, not a store: never persist the queue itself; derive it fresh from chain lugs + claims table on each query
Priority order: initiative_rank (from initiatives/index.json focus_lock > approved > measuring) > chain ROI > created_at
Available chain: status=open AND (claimed_by=null OR expires_at < now())
Atomic claim: query then claim must be done as a single Supabase transaction to prevent race; local fallback uses file lock
Skip-on-claimed: when a queried chain is found claimed by another session, skip it without error — this is expected behavior in multi-Ozi setups

queue_item.priority: int — computed: 100 if focus_lock initiative, 50 if approved initiative, 10 default
queue_item.available: bool — true if unclaimed or claim expired

queue_query_params.filter_available: bool default true — return only claimable chains
queue_query_params.filter_initiative: initiative_id | null — narrow to one initiative

queue_query_response.total_chains: int — total chain lugs in bytype/chain/

constraints: Queue is always derived fresh — never cache the queue for more than one session startup
Claim and query must be atomic when using Supabase (SELECT FOR UPDATE or INSERT with conflict check); a two-step query-then-claim with a gap is a race condition
Priority computation uses initiatives/index.json focus_lock and lifecycle_state — the queue reader must load this file to compute priority correctly
A chain with all children in completed_children[] must be excluded from the queue (status=completed) regardless of claim state
Ozi must not claim more chains than available session budget can complete — query with budget_tokens as a filter hint
tests: Empty queue: no chain lugs exist; queue_query returns available_chains=0, items=[]
Priority ordering: one focus_lock initiative chain + one approved initiative chain; focus_lock chain appears first
Claimed chain excluded: chain has valid (non-expired) claim; query with filter_available=true returns it as unavailable
Expired claim included: chain claim expires_at < now(); query returns it as available with claim_status=expired
Completed chain excluded: chain has all children in completed_children[]; does not appear in queue
Atomic claim race: two agents query simultaneously, both see same unclaimed chain, both attempt to claim — only one succeeds
perceive: Read this spec. Read spec-goal-chain-v1 for chain lug schema. Check WAI-Spoke/WAI-State.json _work_queue for how current queue is implemented. Read WAI-Spoke/initiatives/index.json for priority computation inputs.
execute: 1. Implement queue_query() function: reads bytype/chain/ lugs + wai_claims table, applies filters, computes priority, returns sorted queue_query_response. 2. Add queue_query() call to session startup routine before claiming begins. 3. Add budget_tokens parameter to queue_query() for Ozi dispatch. 4. Implement local fallback (file scan + file lock) for single-Ozi spokes without Supabase. 5. Add queue depth metric to Navigator input for portfolio planning.
verify: 1. queue_query() called with no chain lugs returns available_chains=0. 2. Priority: focus_lock chain ranks above approved chain. 3. Valid claim excluded from available results. 4. Expired claim included in available results. 5. Completed chain excluded. 6. Two simultaneous claims of the same chain — one rejected.
